content: The mean and variance of the data $$4,5,6,6,7,8, x, y$$, where $$x< y$$ are 6 and $$\frac{9}{4}$$, respectively. Then, $$x^2-2 y$$ is equal to
explanation:
$$\text { Given, } \text { mean }=6 \text { and variance }=\frac{9}{4}$$

$$\therefore \frac{4+5+6+6+7+8+x+y}{8}=6$$

$$ \begin{array}{lrl} \Rightarrow & 36+x+y=48 \\ \Rightarrow & x+y=12 \quad \text{... (i)} \end{array}$$

And

$$\frac{1}{8}\left(4^2+5^2+6^2+6^2+7^2+8^2+x^2+y^2\right)-6^2=\frac{9}{4}$$

$$ \begin{array}{rrrr} \Rightarrow & \frac{1}{8}\left(226+x^2+y^2\right) =\frac{153}{4} \\ \Rightarrow & x^2+y^2 =80 \quad \text{... (ii)} \end{array} $$

On solving Eqs. (i) and (ii), we get $$ \begin{aligned} x & =4 \text { and } y=8 \quad [\because x < y]\\ \therefore \quad x^2-2 y & =(4)^2-2(8) \\ & =16-16=0 \end{aligned} $$

content: The mean of five observations is 4 and their variance is 5.2. If three of these observations are 1, 2 and 6, then the other two are
explanation:
Let the two unknown items be x and y. Then,

Mean $$ = 4 \Rightarrow {{1 + 2 + 6 + x + y} \over 5} = 4$$

$$\Rightarrow$$ x + y = 11 ..... (i)

and variance = 5.2

$$ \Rightarrow {{{1^2} + {2^2} + {6^2} + {x^2} + {y^2}} \over 5}$$ $$-$$ (mean)2 = 5.2

$$\Rightarrow$$ 41 + x2 + y2 = 5(5.2 + 16)

$$\Rightarrow$$ 41 + x2 + y2 = 106

$$\Rightarrow$$ x2 + y2 = 65 ..... (ii)

On solving Eqs. (i) and (ii), we get

x = 4, y = 7 or x = 7, y = 4

content: For a random variable X, E(X) = 3 and E(X2) = 11. The variable of X is :
explanation:
Given, E(X) = 3 and E(X2) = 11

Variable of X = E(X2) $$-$$ [E(X)]2 = 11 $$-$$ 32 = 11 $$-$$ 9 = 2

content: The sum of 10 items is 12 and the sum of their squares is 18, then the standard deviation will be
explanation:
Given, x1 + x2 + x3 + .... + x10 = 12

and x$$_1^2$$ + x$$_2^2$$ + ..... + x$$_{10}^2$$ = 18

$${\sigma ^2} = {1 \over n}\sum {{x^2} - {{\left( {{1 \over n}\sum x } \right)}^2}} $$

$$ = {1 \over {10}} \times 18 - {\left( {{1 \over {10}} \times 12} \right)^2} = {9 \over 5} - {{36} \over {25}} = {9 \over {25}}$$

$$\therefore$$ Standard deviation = 3/5.

content:
The runs of two players for 10 innings each are as follows

The more consistent player is
explanation:
Let us make the table from the given data

Player A Player B
$${x_i}$$ $${x_i} - 61$$ $${({x_i} - 61)^2}$$ $${y_i}$$ $${y_i} - 61$$ $${({y_i} - 61)^2}$$
58 $$ - $$3 9 94 33 1089
59 $$ - $$2 4 26 $$ - $$35 1225
60 $$ - $$1 1 92 31 961
54 $$ - $$7 49 65 4 16
65 4 16 96 35 1225
66 5 25 78 17 289
52 $$ - $$9 81 14 $$ - $$47 2209
75 14 196 34 $$ - $$27 729
69 8 64 98 37 1369
52 $$ - $$9 81 13 $$ - $$48 2304
$$\sum {{x_i} = 610} $$ $$\sum {{{({x_i} - 61)}^2} = 526} $$ $$\sum {{y_i} = 610} $$ $$\sum {{{({y_i} - 61)}^2} = 11416} $$

For player A, Mean = $${{\sum {{x_i}} } \over n} = {{610} \over {10}} = 61$$

$$SD = \sqrt {{{\sum {{{({x_i} - 61)}^2}} } \over N}} $$

$$ = \sqrt {{{526} \over {10}}} = 7.25$$

For player B, Mean = $${{\sum {{y_i}} } \over n} = {{610} \over {10}} = 61$$

$$SD = \sqrt {{{\sum {{{({y_i} - 61)}^2}} } \over N}} $$

$$ = \sqrt {{{11416} \over {10}}} = 33.79$$

Since, SD for player A is 7.25 < SD for player B is 33.79.

Hence, player A is more consistent player.

content: The mean of five observation is 5 and their variance is 9.20. If three of the given five observation are 1, 3 and 8, then a ratio of other two observations is
explanation:
Let two observations be x and y.

$$\therefore$$ Mean $$ = {{1 + 3 + 8 + x + y} \over 5} = {{12 + x + y} \over 5}$$

$$ \Rightarrow 5 = {{x + y + 12} \over {15}} \Rightarrow x + y = 13$$ ..... (i)

Now, variance $$ = {{\sum {x_i^2} } \over n} - {(\overline x )^2}$$

$$ \Rightarrow 9.20 = {{{1^2} + {3^2} + {8^2} + {x^2} + {y^2}} \over 5} - 25$$

$$ \Rightarrow 46 = 74 + {x^2} + {y^2} - 125$$

$$ \Rightarrow {x^2} + {y^2} = 97$$ ...... (ii)

From Eqs. (i) and (ii), we get

$${(x + y)^2} - 2xy = 97$$

$$ \Rightarrow {(13)^2} - 2xy = 97$$

$$ \Rightarrow 2xy = 169 - 97 = 72$$

$$\Rightarrow$$ xy = 36

$$\therefore$$ x : y = 4 : 9
